title: jodina rhombifolia subsp. rhombifolia
intro: Jodina rhombifolia subsp. rhombifolia, also known as the Jodina or Stinkwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Jodina rhombifolia subsp. rhombifolia include relief from pain, fever reduction, and the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Jodina rhombifolia subsp. rhombifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
